rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 14, 2021 line 1. NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND APPLICATION BY INTERESTED PARTIES LIN DEE LIU DBA LIN DEE LIU SERVICES, ET AL., FOR PERMISSION TO CONDUCT NON JUDICIAL FORECLOSURE OF 1901 PACIFIC AVENUE AND 1012 PAGE STREET is continued to September 21, 2021 per September 3, 2021 ex-parte order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR TRASVINA VS. GLADYS DEWITT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 14, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT GLADYS DEWITT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant moves to strike allegations asserted in the Second, Third and Fourth causes of action (paragraphs 16, 19/50/65, and 21/51/53) forming the claims for wrongful eviction and tenant harassment. While paragraphs 16, 19 and 21 are incorporated into all causes of action, and allegations identical to paragraph 19 and 21 are separately made only within the second and third causes of action, Defendant moves to strike claims in the Fourth, but not in the First cause of action. As to the allegations in paragraphs 16, Defendant failed to carry its burden and show that any claims within the second, third and fourth causes of action (expressly subject to this motion) arise out of Defendant's activity described in this paragraph. Defendant further failed to carry its burden and show that any claims within the fourth cause of action arise out of any activities described in the paragraphs subject to this motion. Motion is granted as to paragraphs 19/50/65 and 21/51/53, except for the following language: "The police report was incident #200481579. The police refused to provide this report to Plaintiff because Plaintiff was not mentioned in incident report #200481579." Moving party carried its burden to show that claims within the second and the third causes of action arise out of protected activity. Opposing party failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits as litigation privilege applies to the activities as described in the Complaint and as supported by Plaintiff's evidence describing the activities in further detail and Plaintiff's description of the claims in its motion for leave to file supplemental complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UNITY HOMES, INC. VS. GREGORY GEETER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 14, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT GREGORY GEETER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations in the Complaint (which appears to state a cause of action for forcible entry/detainer) and allegations in UD-101 suggesting that a 15 Day Notice to Pay or Quit was served on the defendant. It also appears from the opposition that plaintiff wishes to allege a cause of action for trespass, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend its complaint to allege a cause of action for trespass in a general civil action and to obtain 30- day summons. If Plaintiff wishes to proceed under CCP 1159 and 1160 Plaintiff's complaint and UD-101 must so clearly allege. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAREN L. BANKHEAD VS. ANTHONY L. HYDEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 14, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT ANTHONY HYDEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on termination of license. Moving party failed to show that there is another case pending involving complex issues of title to the property. Furthermore, to the extent Defendant seeks a stay, Defendant did not cite any authority for the proposition that such stay is properly issued by this Court as opposed to the Court where unlimited jurisdiction action is pending. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
